---
title: "Series_Assignment"
author: "Marta Traczyk"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: false
    theme: darkly
execute:
  echo: false
---
```{r}
warning = FALSE

library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
```


# Game of Thrones  

## Title and logo

**Game of Thrones** is an American fantasy drama television series created by *David Benioff* and *D. B. Weiss* for **HBO**. It is the first adaptation of the *A Song of Ice and Fire* franchise, a series of high fantasy novels by *George R. R. Martin*, the first of which is *A Game of Thrones*. The show premiered on **HBO** in the United States on April 17, 2011, and concluded on May 19, 2019, with 73 episodes broadcast over eight seasons.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("Game_of_Thrones_title_card.jpg")
```
## Summary of statistics

```{r}
data <- read.csv("season_1.csv")
```

```{r}
summary_stats <- data %>%
  summarise(
    mean_viewers = mean(viewers, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_viewers = median(viewers, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_viewers = min(viewers, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_viewers = max(viewers, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_viewers = sd(viewers, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

kable(summary_stats, caption = "Summary Statistics of Viewership")
```


The summary statistics of episode viewership show that the average number of viewers is approximately **2.52** million, while the median is slightly lower at **2.45** million. This suggests that the distribution of viewership is relatively balanced, with no extreme skewness. The minimum viewership recorded is **2.20** million, and the maximum reaches **3.04** million, indicating a moderate range of audience size across episodes.


```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x = premiere_date, y = viewers_change)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Episode-to-Episode Change in Viewership",
    x = "Premiere Date",
    y = "Change in Viewers (millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),   # removes date labels
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()   # removes tick marks
  )
```

The graph shows that episode-to-episode changes in viewership fluctuate around zero, indicating relatively stable audience numbers with only small increases or decreases between episodes. While there are a few noticeable spikes, overall the variation is moderate rather than extreme.
